  (5, 503, '2026-05-06', 60),(6, 504, '2026-05-06', 90),
  (7, 505, '2026-05-06', 220),(8, 506, '2026-05-06', 30);
CREATE TABLE user_state_master (
    user_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    last_active_date DATE, is_active_l28 INTEGER, total_ltv NUMERIC
);
INSERT INTO user_state_master VALUES
  (501, '2026-05-05', 1, 1450.00),
  (502, '2026-04-01', 0,  120.00),
  (503, '2026-04-30', 1,  600.00),
  (504, '2026-04-12', 1,  330.00),
  (507, '2026-05-04', 1,  900.00),
  (508, '2026-05-03', 1,  250.00),
  (509, '2026-01-15', 0, 1500.00);

WITH today_activity AS (
    SELECT user_id, SUM(amount) AS today_spend, COUNT(order_id) AS today_qty
    FROM orders WHERE order_date = DATE '2026-05-06'
    GROUP BY user_id
),
growth_merge AS (
    SELECT
        COALESCE(t.user_id, s.user_id) AS user_id,
        t.today_spend, t.today_qty,
        s.last_active_date, s.is_active_l28,
        CASE
            WHEN s.user_id IS NULL THEN 'NEW'
            WHEN t.user_id IS NOT NULL AND COALESCE(s.is_active_l28,0) = 1 THEN 'RETAINED'
            WHEN t.user_id IS NOT NULL AND COALESCE(s.is_active_l28,0) = 0 THEN 'RESURRECTED'
            WHEN t.user_id IS NULL AND COALESCE(s.is_active_l28,0) = 1 THEN 'CHURNED'
            ELSE 'STALE'
        END AS growth_status,
        CASE WHEN t.user_id IS NOT NULL THEN 0
             ELSE (DATE '2026-05-06' - s.last_active_date)
        END AS days_since_active
    FROM user_state_master s
    FULL OUTER JOIN today_activity t ON s.user_id = t.user_id
)
SELECT growth_status,
    COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS user_count,
    COALESCE(ROUND(SUM(today_spend), 2), 0) AS total_revenue,
    COALESCE(SUM(today_qty), 0) AS total_qty,
    CASE WHEN COALESCE(SUM(today_spend), 0) = 0 THEN NULL
         ELSE ROUND(SUM(today_spend) / COUNT(DISTINCT user_id), 2) END AS arpu,
    COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FILTER (WHERE days_since_active = 0) AS l1_users,
    COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FILTER (WHERE days_since_active <= 7) AS l7_users,
    COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FILTER (WHERE days_since_active <= 28) AS l28_users
FROM growth_merge
WHERE growth_status <> 'STALE'
GROUP BY growth_status
ORDER BY CASE growth_status
    WHEN 'NEW' THEN 1 WHEN 'RETAINED' THEN 2
    WHEN 'RESURRECTED' THEN 3 WHEN 'CHURNED' THEN 4
END;

title: NTILE Decile Velocity Cohorts — Exact Top-10 % vs PERCENT_RANK Continuous Rank
schema: orders(order_id, customer_id, order_date, amount, status), params(order_id, customer_id, order_date, amount)
scenario: Marketing wants exact top-10 % / next-10 % / … cohort splits for the dispatcher dashboard — not 'roughly the top decile' but 'exactly 10 % of customers, no more, no less'. The right function is NTILE(10), which guarantees equally-sized buckets even on skewed distributions where PERCENT_RANK would clump everyone at the top. The cohort drives priority queue in support, ad re-targeting budgets, and the L1/L7 dashboards.
question:
Snapshot 2025-04-01. Per customer with at least 2 paid orders in the last 90 days, compute:
  customer_id, l90_orders, l90_spend,
  velocity_decile     — NTILE(10) over l90_spend DESC (1 = top 10 %)
  pct_rank            — PERCENT_RANK over l90_spend DESC
  cohort              — 'top_10' if decile = 1,
                        'next_15' if decile in (2,3) and pct_rank ≤ 0.25,
                        'mid_50'  if decile in (3..7),
                        'bottom_25' otherwise.

Sort by velocity_decile ASC, l90_spend DESC.
solution:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS orders;
CREATE TABLE orders (
    order_id    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    customer_id INTEGER,
    order_date  TEXT,
    amount      REAL
);
-- 20 customers spanning a wide spend distribution so NTILE produces
-- visibly different bucket sizes from PERCENT_RANK.
INSERT INTO orders VALUES
  (1, 1,'2025-03-28',2000),(2, 1,'2025-03-15',1800),(3, 1,'2025-02-28',1900),
  (4, 2,'2025-03-25',1500),(5, 2,'2025-03-10',1600),
  (6, 3,'2025-03-22',1300),(7, 3,'2025-03-01',1200),
  (8, 4,'2025-03-20',1100),(9, 4,'2025-02-25',1000),
  (10,5,'2025-03-18', 900),(11,5,'2025-02-20', 950),
  (12,6,'2025-03-15', 800),(13,6,'2025-02-15', 850),
  (14,7,'2025-03-12', 700),(15,7,'2025-02-10', 750),
  (16,8,'2025-03-09', 600),(17,8,'2025-02-05', 650),
  (18,9,'2025-03-06', 500),(19,9,'2025-01-28', 550),
  (20,10,'2025-03-03', 400),(21,10,'2025-01-25', 450),
  (22,11,'2025-03-01', 350),(23,11,'2025-01-22', 380),
  (24,12,'2025-02-26', 300),(25,12,'2025-01-19', 320),
  (26,13,'2025-02-23', 250),(27,13,'2025-01-16', 270),
  (28,14,'2025-02-20', 200),(29,14,'2025-01-13', 220),
  (30,15,'2025-02-17', 170),(31,15,'2025-01-10', 180),
  (32,16,'2025-02-14', 140),(33,16,'2025-01-07', 150),
  (34,17,'2025-02-11', 110),(35,17,'2025-01-04', 120),
  (36,18,'2025-02-08',  90),(37,18,'2025-01-01', 100),
  (38,19,'2025-02-05',  60),(39,19,'2024-12-29',  70),
  (40,20,'2025-02-02',  30),(41,20,'2024-12-26',  40);

-- ── PERF rationale ───────────────────────────────────────
--   NTILE(10) is the right pick here, NOT PERCENT_RANK:
--     • NTILE guarantees equally-sized buckets — the 'top 10 %'
--       cohort always has ceil(n/10) customers, which is what the
--       support-prioritisation budget assumes.
--     • PERCENT_RANK is continuous and is great for relative ranking
--       (RFM Q1 uses it to compute composite tiers), but on a long-
--       tailed distribution it can put 30 % of customers above 0.90
--       — useless for cohort budgets.
--     • A senior tip: ALWAYS show both deciles AND a continuous
--       percentile so the consumer can pick the framing that matches
--       the decision (decile for fixed budget, pct for trend).
--   Date-range WHERE prunes the partition; we never scan history.
-- ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
WITH params(snapshot) AS (VALUES ('2025-04-01')),
agg AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        COUNT(*)              AS l90_orders,
        ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS l90_spend
    FROM orders
    WHERE order_date >= date((SELECT snapshot FROM params), '-90 days')
      AND order_date <  (SELECT snapshot FROM params)
    GROUP BY customer_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id, l90_orders, l90_spend,
        NTILE(10)      OVER (ORDER BY l90_spend DESC) AS velocity_decile,
        ROUND(PERCENT_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY l90_spend ASC), 3)
            AS pct_rank
    FROM agg
)
SELECT
    customer_id, l90_orders, l90_spend,
    velocity_decile,
    pct_rank,
    CASE
        WHEN velocity_decile = 1                                 THEN 'top_10'
        WHEN velocity_decile IN (2, 3) AND pct_rank >= 0.75      THEN 'next_15'
        WHEN velocity_decile BETWEEN 3 AND 7                     THEN 'mid_50'
        ELSE                                                          'bottom_25'
    END AS cohort
FROM ranked
ORDER BY velocity_decile ASC, l90_spend DESC;

title: Salting a Skewed Aggregation — The Classic Pareto-Distribution Rescue
scenario: Most e-commerce customers follow a Pareto distribution: the top 1 % of customer_ids own 40-60 % of the rows. A naive `GROUP BY customer_id` ships every row of one mega-account to a single reducer — the job runs for hours waiting on that one task while the other reducers sit idle. The senior rescue is SALTING: bolt a small random integer onto the group key so the mega-account's rows spread across N reducers, do a partial aggregate, then roll the partials up in a second tiny pass. Same answer, balanced shuffle.
question:
Compute total_spend per customer, but write the query in the salted two-stage form so the shuffle is balanced even when one customer owns most of the rows.

  Stage 1: GROUP BY (customer_id, salt) where salt is a small
           uniform integer in [0, N). N = 16 is a sensible
           default (one salt bucket per CPU on a typical worker).
  Stage 2: GROUP BY customer_id over the partials.

Output: customer_id, salt_buckets_seen, total_spend.
Sort by total_spend DESC.
solution:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS orders;
CREATE TABLE orders (
    order_id    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    customer_id INTEGER,
    amount      NUMERIC
);
-- Pareto: customer 1 has 30 rows, the long tail has 1-3 rows each.
INSERT INTO orders VALUES
  (1,1,100),(2,1,110),(3,1,120),(4,1,130),(5,1,140),
  (6,1,150),(7,1,160),(8,1,170),(9,1,180),(10,1,190),
  (11,1,200),(12,1,210),(13,1,220),(14,1,230),(15,1,240),
  (16,1,250),(17,1,260),(18,1,270),(19,1,280),(20,1,290),
  (21,1,300),(22,1,310),(23,1,320),(24,1,330),(25,1,340),
  (26,1,350),(27,1,360),(28,1,370),(29,1,380),(30,1,390),
  (31,2,500),(32,2,520),
  (33,3,400),
  (34,4,300),(35,4,310),(36,4,330),
  (37,5,200),
  (38,6,100),(39,6,110);

-- ╭───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
-- │  Salt = uniform int in [0, 16). FLOOR(RANDOM() * 16)::INT │
-- │  is the portable Postgres form. The two-stage aggregation │
-- │  spreads the hot key across 16 reducers.                  │
-- ╰───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
WITH salted AS (
    SELECT customer_id,
           FLOOR(RANDOM() * 16)::INT AS salt,
           amount
    FROM orders
),
partial_agg AS (
    SELECT customer_id, salt,
           SUM(amount) AS partial_sum,
           COUNT(*)    AS partial_rows
    FROM salted
    GROUP BY customer_id, salt
)
SELECT customer_id,
       COUNT(*)                   AS salt_buckets_seen,
       ROUND(SUM(partial_sum), 2) AS total_spend
FROM partial_agg
GROUP BY customer_id
ORDER BY total_spend DESC;

title: DAU / MAU With APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT — HyperLogLog at Scale
scenario: Daily and Monthly Active Users (DAU / MAU) are the headline engagement metric, but COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) on a billions-of-rows event table forces a full sort or hash that materialises every distinct id in memory. APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT (HyperLogLog) trades a typical 1-2 % relative error for constant-memory aggregation: the sketch is a tiny fixed-size byte array regardless of cardinality. For a daily exec dashboard the trade is obvious — nobody noticed the third decimal of DAU but everybody noticed the dashboard not rendering by 9 AM.
question:
Build a daily report for the past 7 days with one row per day:
  event_date | dau_exact | dau_approx | absolute_error
Show the same query in two flavours — the exact COUNT(DISTINCT) for small / medium tables, and the APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT (or BigQuery's APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT, Spark's approx_count_distinct, Snowflake's HLL) variant for billion-row tables. SQLite has no native HLL; use the exact count as the runnable result and document the production form inline.
solution:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS events;
CREATE TABLE events (
    user_id  INTEGER,
    event_ts DATE
);
-- Postgres-native: explode 7 days × 700 events using generate_series.
INSERT INTO events
SELECT (FLOOR(RANDOM() * 5000) + 1)::INT, d::DATE
FROM generate_series(DATE '2025-03-25', DATE '2025-03-31', INTERVAL '1 day') AS d
CROSS JOIN generate_series(1, 100);

-- ╭───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
-- │  PERF rationale —                                          │
-- │    COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) builds a hash set of every     │
-- │    unique id in memory. For billions of rows this is the   │
-- │    single most expensive op. APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT (HLL)   │
-- │    estimates with O(log log n) memory and ~2% error.       │
-- │  PGlite doesn't ship HLL out of the box, so this query    │
-- │  shows the EXACT count alongside what you'd swap for HLL  │
-- │  in production (Snowflake/BigQuery/Redshift/Trino).       │
-- ╰───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
SELECT
    event_ts AS event_date,
    COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS dau_exact,
    -- Production swap-in:
    --   APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT(user_id)  AS dau_approx
    COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS dau_approx,
    0                       AS absolute_error
FROM events
GROUP BY event_ts
ORDER BY event_ts;

title: Top-3 Products per Customer — QUALIFY Filter on a Window Result
subtopic: QUALIFY · top-N per group · window-result filtering
schema: order_items(order_id, customer_id, product_id, qty, unit_price, per_product, ranked)
scenario: Top-N-per-group is one of the most-asked patterns in real analytics work. The senior shape uses QUALIFY (Snowflake / BigQuery / Databricks SQL / Teradata) — a clause that filters on a window-function result without an extra subquery. Engines that lack QUALIFY (Postgres, MySQL, SQLite) need a wrapping CTE + WHERE on ROW_NUMBER. Both deliver the same answer; QUALIFY is the cleaner read and the planner doesn't have to materialise the intermediate.
question:
Per customer, return their top 3 products by total spend (qty × unit_price summed across orders):
  customer_id, product_id, total_spend, rk
where rk is the dense rank within the customer's product list. Show the SQLite-runnable form (CTE + WHERE rk ≤ 3) and document the QUALIFY equivalent inline. Sort by customer_id ASC, rk ASC.
solution:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS order_items;
CREATE TABLE order_items (
    order_id    INTEGER,
    customer_id INTEGER,
    product_id  INTEGER,
    qty         INTEGER,
    unit_price  NUMERIC
);
INSERT INTO order_items VALUES
  -- C1: 5 different products
  (1,1,101,2,30.0),(2,1,101,1,30.0),
  (3,1,102,1,80.0),
  (4,1,103,3,15.0),
  (5,1,104,5,5.0),
  (6,1,105,1,40.0),
  -- C2: 4 products
  (7,2,201,2,50.0),(8,2,201,1,50.0),
  (9,2,202,1,100.0),
  (10,2,203,4,10.0),
  (11,2,204,2,25.0);

-- ╭───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
-- │  Top-3 products per customer using DENSE_RANK on the      │
-- │  total spend per (customer, product). ROUND on NUMERIC    │
-- │  works in Postgres natively.                              │
-- ╰───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
WITH per_product AS (
    SELECT customer_id, product_id,
           ROUND(SUM(qty * unit_price), 2) AS total_spend
    FROM order_items
    GROUP BY customer_id, product_id
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT customer_id, product_id, total_spend,
           DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id
                              ORDER BY total_spend DESC) AS rk
    FROM per_product
)
SELECT customer_id, product_id, total_spend, rk
FROM ranked
WHERE rk <= 3
ORDER BY customer_id ASC, rk ASC;

title: Top-3 Titles per Region by Watch Hours
solution:
SELECT region, title_id, hours
FROM (
  SELECT region, title_id,
    SUM(watch_minutes) / 60.0 AS hours,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY region ORDER BY SUM(watch_minutes) DESC) AS rk
  FROM plays
  GROUP BY region, title_id
) t
WHERE rk <= 3
ORDER BY region, hours DESC;

title: Free Trial to Paid Conversion Funnel
schema: subscriptions(user_id, signup_date, trial_end_date, first_paid_date, cancel_date, subscription_id, customer_id, plan, start_date, end_date, status)
solution:
SELECT
  DATE_TRUNC('week', signup_date::date)::date AS cohort_week,
  COUNT(*) AS signups,
  COUNT(first_paid_date) AS converted_to_paid,
  SUM(CASE WHEN first_paid_date IS NULL AND cancel_date <= trial_end_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS dropped_in_trial,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(first_paid_date) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS trial_to_paid_pct
FROM subscriptions
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('week', signup_date::date)
ORDER BY cohort_week;

title: Bingewatch Detection — 3+ Episodes of Same Show in 24h
solution:
WITH counted AS (
  SELECT a.user_id, a.show_id, a.episode_id, a.started_at,
    COUNT(*) AS eps_in_last_24h
  FROM episode_plays a
  JOIN episode_plays b
    ON b.user_id = a.user_id
   AND b.show_id = a.show_id
   AND julianday(b.started_at) BETWEEN julianday(a.started_at) - 1 AND julianday(a.started_at)
  GROUP BY a.user_id, a.show_id, a.episode_id, a.started_at
)
SELECT DISTINCT user_id, show_id
FROM counted
WHERE eps_in_last_24h >= 2;

title: Auto-Play Conversion Rate per Title
solution:
SELECT title_id,
  COUNT(*) AS impressions,
  SUM(CASE WHEN user_continued = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS continued,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN user_continued = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS continue_rate_pct
FROM auto_play_events
GROUP BY title_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
ORDER BY continue_rate_pct DESC;

title: Rebuffering Rate by ISP — QoE Analysis
solution:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS qoe;
CREATE TABLE qoe (session_id INT, isp TEXT, watched_seconds INT, rebuf_seconds INT, rebuf_count INT);
INSERT INTO qoe (session_id, isp, watched_seconds, rebuf_seconds, rebuf_count) VALUES
  (1,'Comcast',1800,30,2),
  (2,'Comcast',2400,60,3),
  (3,'Comcast',1500,15,1),
  (4,'Verizon',3000,10,1),
  (5,'Verizon',2700,20,2),
  (6,'Verizon',1200,5,1),
  (7,'AT&T',2000,80,4),
  (8,'AT&T',1500,90,5),
  (9,'Spectrum',2500,25,1),
  (10,'Spectrum',1800,18,2);
SELECT isp,
  COUNT(*) AS sessions,
  ROUND(SUM(rebuf_seconds) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(watched_seconds + rebuf_seconds), 0), 3) AS rebuf_pct,
  ROUND(AVG(rebuf_count), 2) AS avg_rebufs_per_session
FROM qoe
GROUP BY isp
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
ORDER BY rebuf_pct DESC;

title: Concurrent-Stream Cap Violation per Account
solution:
WITH events AS (
  SELECT account_id, started_at AS t, +1 AS delta FROM streams
  UNION ALL
  SELECT account_id, ended_at,           -1        FROM streams
),
active AS (
  SELECT account_id, t,
    SUM(delta) OVER (PARTITION BY account_id ORDER BY t ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING) AS concurrent
  FROM events
)
SELECT DISTINCT account_id
FROM active
WHERE concurrent > 1;

title: Title Launch Curve — D7 vs D28 Watch Hours
solution:
SELECT title_id, launched_on,
  ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN (played_at - launched_on) <= INTERVAL '7 days'  THEN watch_minutes END) / 60.0, 1) AS d7_hours,
  ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN (played_at - launched_on) <= INTERVAL '28 days' THEN watch_minutes END) / 60.0, 1) AS d28_hours,
  ROUND(100.0 *
    SUM(CASE WHEN (played_at - launched_on) <= INTERVAL '7 days'  THEN watch_minutes END) /
    NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN (played_at - launched_on) <= INTERVAL '28 days' THEN watch_minutes END), 0),
    1) AS first_week_share_pct
FROM title_plays
GROUP BY title_id, launched_on
ORDER BY d28_hours DESC;

title: Search-then-Play Attribution Within Session
schema: searches(user_id, search_ts, query_text, top_title_id), plays(user_id, play_ts, title_id)
solution:
SELECT s.query_text,
  COUNT(*) AS searches,
  COUNT(p.title_id) AS resulting_plays,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(p.title_id) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS search_to_play_pct
FROM searches s
LEFT JOIN plays p
  ON p.user_id  = s.user_id
 AND p.title_id = s.top_title_id
 AND EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (p.play_ts - s.search_ts)) / 86400.0 BETWEEN 0 AND 7
GROUP BY s.query_text
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1
ORDER BY search_to_play_pct DESC;

title: Cohort Retention by Signup Week — Week-N Active Rate
schema: users(user_id, signup_date, name, email, country), activity(user_id, activity_date)
solution:
WITH cohorts AS (
  SELECT user_id, DATE_TRUNC('week', signup_date::date)::date AS cohort_week
  FROM users
),
dedup AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT user_id, DATE_TRUNC('week', activity_date::date)::date AS active_week
  FROM activity
),
joined AS (
  SELECT c.cohort_week, d.active_week,
         ((d.active_week - c.cohort_week) / 7) AS week_n,
         c.user_id
  FROM cohorts c JOIN dedup d USING (user_id)
)
SELECT cohort_week, week_n,
  COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS active_users,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) /
    FIRST_VALUE(COUNT(DISTINCT user_id)) OVER (PARTITION BY cohort_week ORDER BY week_n), 1) AS retention_pct
FROM joined
WHERE week_n BETWEEN 0 AND 12
GROUP BY cohort_week, week_n
ORDER BY cohort_week, week_n;

title: Profile-Level Watch Distribution per Account
solution:
SELECT account_id, profile_id,
  SUM(watch_minutes) AS profile_minutes,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(watch_minutes) /
    SUM(SUM(watch_minutes)) OVER (PARTITION BY account_id), 1) AS share_pct,
  RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY account_id ORDER BY SUM(watch_minutes) DESC) AS profile_rank
FROM plays
GROUP BY account_id, profile_id
ORDER BY account_id, share_pct DESC;

title: Late-Arriving Dimensions — Inferred-Member Pattern
subtopic: Late-arriving dimensions, inferred members, defensive joins
schema: viewing_events(view_id, content_id, watch_time_seconds), content_dim(content_id, title, genre, effective_date)
solution:
WITH current_content AS (
  SELECT content_id, title, genre,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY content_id
      ORDER BY COALESCE(effective_date, '9999-12-31') DESC
    ) AS rn
  FROM content_dim
)
SELECT v.view_id,
  COALESCE(c.title, 'Pending Metadata Update') AS title,
  COALESCE(c.genre, 'Unknown') AS genre,
  v.watch_time_seconds
FROM viewing_events v
LEFT JOIN current_content c
  ON v.content_id = c.content_id AND c.rn = 1;

title: Binge-Watching Streak — 3+ Consecutive Days of Viewing
solution:
WITH daily_views AS (
  SELECT user_id, view_date
  FROM user_views
  GROUP BY user_id, view_date
),
islands AS (
  SELECT user_id, view_date,
    julianday(view_date) - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY view_date) AS island_group
  FROM daily_views
),
streak_counts AS (
  SELECT user_id,
    MIN(view_date) AS streak_start,
    MAX(view_date) AS streak_end,
    COUNT(*)       AS consecutive_days
  FROM islands
  GROUP BY user_id, island_group
)
SELECT *
FROM streak_counts
WHERE consecutive_days >= 2
ORDER BY consecutive_days DESC;

title: Content Gap — 30-Day Churntential Inactivity Window
solution:
WITH user_activity AS (
  SELECT user_id, view_date,
    LEAD(view_date) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY view_date) AS next_view_date
  FROM (SELECT DISTINCT user_id, view_date FROM user_views) t
)
SELECT user_id,
  view_date AS gap_start_date,
  next_view_date AS gap_end_date,
  (next_view_date::date - view_date::date) AS gap_duration
FROM user_activity
WHERE (next_view_date::date - view_date::date) > 5;

title: LAD-Aware Binge Detection — 3+ Episodes of Same Series in One Day
schema: fact_viewing_history(user_id, series_id, episode_id, view_date), dim_series_metadata(series_id, series_title, season_number, effective_date)
solution:
WITH current_metadata AS (
  SELECT series_id, series_title, season_number
  FROM (
    SELECT *,
      ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY series_id ORDER BY effective_date DESC) AS rn
    FROM dim_series_metadata
  ) m
  WHERE rn = 1
),
daily_binges AS (
  SELECT v.user_id, v.series_id, v.view_date,
    COUNT(v.episode_id) AS episodes_watched,
    COALESCE(m.series_title, 'Series ID: ' || v.series_id || ' (Pending Metadata)') AS series_display_name
  FROM fact_viewing_history v
  LEFT JOIN current_metadata m USING (series_id)
  GROUP BY v.user_id, v.series_id, v.view_date, m.series_title
)
SELECT *
FROM daily_binges
WHERE episodes_watched >= 3
ORDER BY view_date DESC, episodes_watched DESC;

title: Driver Utilization Rate — Time-on-Trip / Time-Online
schema: driver_shifts(driver_id, online_at, offline_at), trips(driver_id, started_at, ended_at)
solution:
SELECT s.driver_id,
  ROUND(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (s.offline_at - s.online_at)) / 86400.0) * 24.0, 2) AS hours_online,
  ROUND(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (t.ended_at - t.started_at)) / 86400.0) * 24.0, 2) AS hours_on_trip,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (t.ended_at - t.started_at)) / 86400.0)
              / NULLIF(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (s.offline_at - s.online_at)) / 86400.0), 0), 1) AS utilization_pct
FROM driver_shifts s
LEFT JOIN trips t ON t.driver_id = s.driver_id
GROUP BY s.driver_id
ORDER BY utilization_pct DESC;

title: Surge Multiplier vs Ride Volume per Area
solution:
SELECT area_id,
  CASE
    WHEN surge_multiplier <  1.5 THEN 'normal'
    WHEN surge_multiplier <  2.0 THEN 'mild_surge'
    ELSE 'heavy_surge'
  END AS surge_band,
  COUNT(*)        AS rides,
  ROUND(AVG(fare), 2) AS avg_fare,
  ROUND(SUM(fare), 2) AS total_fare
FROM rides
GROUP BY area_id, surge_band
ORDER BY area_id, surge_band;

title: Cancellation Reason Distribution by Side
solution:
SELECT cancelled_by, reason,
  COUNT(*) AS cancellations,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (PARTITION BY cancelled_by), 1) AS pct_of_side
FROM cancellations
GROUP BY cancelled_by, reason
ORDER BY cancelled_by, cancellations DESC;

title: ETA Accuracy — Predicted vs Actual by City
solution:
SELECT city,
  COUNT(*) AS rides,
  ROUND(AVG(ABS(predicted_minutes - actual_minutes)), 1) AS mae_minutes,
  ROUND(100.0 * AVG(ABS(predicted_minutes - actual_minutes) * 1.0
                  / NULLIF(actual_minutes, 0)), 1) AS mape_pct,
  ROUND(AVG(predicted_minutes - actual_minutes), 1) AS mean_bias_minutes
FROM trip_eta
GROUP BY city
ORDER BY mape_pct DESC;

title: Repeat Rider Cohort — 3+ Rides in 30 Days
solution:
SELECT rider_id, COUNT(*) AS rides_30d
FROM rides
WHERE started_at::date >= DATE '2024-02-01' - INTERVAL '30 days'
  AND started_at::date <= DATE '2024-02-01'
GROUP BY rider_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY rides_30d DESC;

title: Round-Trip Detection — Same-Day Return Rides
solution:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS rides;
CREATE TABLE rides (
  ride_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  rider_id INT,
  started_at TIMESTAMP,
  origin_zone TEXT,
  dest_zone TEXT
);
INSERT INTO rides (ride_id, rider_id, started_at, origin_zone, dest_zone) VALUES
  (1, 10, '2025-03-01 08:00:00', 'A', 'B'),
  (2, 10, '2025-03-01 18:00:00', 'B', 'A'),
  (3, 11, '2025-03-02 09:00:00', 'C', 'D'),
  (4, 11, '2025-03-02 14:00:00', 'D', 'C'),
  (5, 12, '2025-03-03 07:00:00', 'E', 'F'),
  (6, 12, '2025-03-04 10:00:00', 'F', 'E'),
  (7, 13, '2025-03-05 06:00:00', 'G', 'H'),
  (8, 13, '2025-03-05 21:00:00', 'H', 'G'),
  (9, 14, '2025-03-06 12:00:00', 'I', 'J');

SELECT a.rider_id, a.ride_id AS first_ride, b.ride_id AS second_ride,
  a.origin_zone || ' -> ' || a.dest_zone AS first_leg,
  b.origin_zone || ' -> ' || b.dest_zone AS second_leg,
  CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (b.started_at - a.started_at)) / 86400.0) * 24 AS INTEGER) AS hours_between
FROM rides a
JOIN rides b
  ON b.rider_id = a.rider_id
 AND b.ride_id != a.ride_id
 AND (b.started_at) > (a.started_at)
 AND (b.started_at - a.started_at) <= INTERVAL '1 days'
ORDER BY a.rider_id, hours_between
LIMIT 20;

title: Driver Tier Earnings Decile — NTILE Distribution
solution:
SELECT driver_id, gross, tips, bonus, gross + tips + bonus AS total_earnings,
  NTILE(10) OVER (ORDER BY gross + tips + bonus DESC) AS earnings_decile
FROM driver_weeks
ORDER BY total_earnings DESC;

title: Demand-Supply Gap — Unfilled Requests per Area
solution:
SELECT area_id,
  COUNT(*) AS total_requests,
  SUM(CASE WHEN fulfilled::text = '1' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fulfilled,
  SUM(CASE WHEN fulfilled::text = '0' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS unfilled,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN fulfilled::text = '0' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS unfill_rate_pct
FROM ride_requests
GROUP BY area_id
ORDER BY unfill_rate_pct DESC;

title: Feed Dwell Time per Post Type
solution:
SELECT post_type,
  COUNT(*) AS views,
  COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unique_viewers,
  ROUND(AVG(dwell_seconds), 1) AS avg_dwell,
  ROUND(SUM(dwell_seconds) * 100.0
      / SUM(SUM(dwell_seconds)) OVER (), 1) AS share_of_total_time_pct
FROM feed_views
GROUP BY post_type
ORDER BY share_of_total_time_pct DESC;

title: Friend-of-Friend Recommendations — 2-Hop Graph
solution:
WITH pairs AS (
  SELECT a.user_id AS me, b.friend_id AS suggested_friend,
    COUNT(*) AS mutual_friends
  FROM friendships a
  JOIN friendships b
    ON a.friend_id = b.user_id
   AND a.user_id != b.friend_id
  GROUP BY a.user_id, b.friend_id
)
SELECT * FROM pairs
ORDER BY mutual_friends DESC, me, suggested_friend
LIMIT 20;

title: Story 24-Hour Engagement Decay
solution:
SELECT
  CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (viewed_at - posted_at)) / 86400.0) * 24 AS INTEGER) AS hours_since_post,
  COUNT(*) AS views
FROM story_views
WHERE EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (viewed_at - posted_at)) / 86400.0 BETWEEN 0 AND 1
GROUP BY hours_since_post
ORDER BY hours_since_post;

title: Ad Click-Through-Rate by Placement
solution:
SELECT placement,
  COUNT(*) AS impressions,
  SUM(CASE WHEN clicked::text = '1' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS clicks,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN clicked::text = '1' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 3) AS ctr_pct
FROM ad_events
GROUP BY placement
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5
ORDER BY ctr_pct DESC;

title: Group Engagement Pyramid — Posters / Commenters / Lurkers
solution:
WITH per_user AS (
  SELECT group_id, user_id,
    SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'post'    THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS posts,
    SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'comment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS comments,
    SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'reaction' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS reactions
  FROM group_actions
  GROUP BY group_id, user_id
)
SELECT group_id,
  SUM(CASE WHEN posts >= 1                THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS posters,
  SUM(CASE WHEN posts = 0 AND comments >= 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS commenters,
  SUM(CASE WHEN posts = 0 AND comments = 0 AND reactions >= 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS reactors,
  COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS active_members
FROM per_user
GROUP BY group_id
ORDER BY active_members DESC;

title: Comment Thread Depth — Recursive Reply Chain
solution:
WITH RECURSIVE chain AS (
  SELECT comment_id, parent_id, post_id, 1 AS depth
  FROM comments WHERE parent_id IS NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT c.comment_id, c.parent_id, c.post_id, ch.depth + 1
  FROM comments c JOIN chain ch ON c.parent_id = ch.comment_id
)
SELECT post_id,
  MAX(depth) AS max_depth,
  COUNT(*)   AS total_comments
FROM chain
GROUP BY post_id
ORDER BY max_depth DESC;

title: Reels Watch-Completion Buckets
solution:
WITH classified AS (
  SELECT *,
    CASE
      WHEN watched_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(total_seconds, 0) >= 0.95 THEN 'completed'
      WHEN watched_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(total_seconds, 0) >= 0.50 THEN 'engaged'
      WHEN watched_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(total_seconds, 0) >= 0.10 THEN 'sampled'
      ELSE 'bounced'
    END AS engagement_band
  FROM reel_plays
)
SELECT engagement_band,
  COUNT(*) AS plays,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 1) AS pct
FROM classified
GROUP BY engagement_band
ORDER BY plays DESC;

title: Message Read Latency — Sent-to-Read Time per Conversation
solution:
SELECT conversation_id,
  COUNT(*) AS messages,
  COUNT(read_at) AS read_messages,
  ROUND(AVG((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (read_at - sent_at)) / 86400.0) * 1440), 1) AS avg_read_minutes,
  ROUND(MAX((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (read_at - sent_at)) / 86400.0) * 1440), 1) AS max_read_minutes
FROM messages
WHERE read_at IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY conversation_id
HAVING COUNT(read_at) >= 2
ORDER BY avg_read_minutes DESC;

title: Search CTR by Result Position
solution:
SELECT position,
  COUNT(*) AS impressions,
  SUM(CASE WHEN clicked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN clicked::int ELSE 0 END) AS clicks,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN clicked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN clicked::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS ctr_pct
FROM search_results
GROUP BY position
ORDER BY position;

title: Query Reformulation — Same Session Different Query
solution:
WITH ordered AS (
  SELECT session_id, search_id, query_text, search_at,
    LAG(query_text) OVER (PARTITION BY session_id ORDER BY search_at) AS prev_query
  FROM searches
)
SELECT session_id,
  COUNT(*) AS searches_in_session,
  SUM(CASE WHEN prev_query IS NOT NULL AND prev_query <> query_text THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS reformulations
FROM ordered
GROUP BY session_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
ORDER BY reformulations DESC;

title: Autocomplete Acceptance Rate per Prefix
solution:
SELECT prefix_length,
  COUNT(*) AS shows,
  SUM(CASE WHEN accepted::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN accepted::int ELSE 0 END) AS accepts,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN accepted::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN accepted::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS acceptance_pct
FROM autocomplete_events
GROUP BY prefix_length
ORDER BY prefix_length;

title: YouTube Watch-Completion per Video
solution:
SELECT video_id,
  COUNT(*) AS views,
  ROUND(AVG(watched_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(total_seconds, 0)) * 100.0, 1) AS avg_completion_pct,
  ROUND(SUM(watched_seconds) / 60.0, 1) AS total_minutes_watched
FROM yt_views
GROUP BY video_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
ORDER BY avg_completion_pct DESC;

title: Ad Keyword Match-Type ROI
schema: ad_keywords(keyword_id, match_type, impressions, clicks, cost, revenue)
solution:
SELECT match_type,
  SUM(impressions) AS impressions,
  SUM(clicks)      AS clicks,
  ROUND(SUM(cost), 2)    AS spend,
  ROUND(SUM(revenue), 2) AS revenue,
  ROUND(SUM(revenue) * 1.0 / NULLIF(SUM(cost), 0), 2) AS roas
FROM ad_keywords
GROUP BY match_type
ORDER BY roas DESC;

title: Maps ETA Bias by Travel Mode
solution:
SELECT mode,
  COUNT(*) AS trips,
  ROUND(AVG(predicted_minutes - actual_minutes), 2)             AS mean_bias,
  ROUND(AVG(ABS(predicted_minutes - actual_minutes)), 2)        AS mae,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN predicted_minutes > actual_minutes THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS pct_overestimated
FROM maps_eta
GROUP BY mode
ORDER BY mae DESC;

title: GA Funnel Drop-Off — Step-to-Step Conversion
solution:
WITH counts AS (
  SELECT step, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS users
  FROM funnel GROUP BY step
)
SELECT step, users,
  LAG(users) OVER (ORDER BY step) AS prev_users,
  ROUND(100.0 * users * 1.0 / NULLIF(LAG(users) OVER (ORDER BY step), 0), 1) AS step_to_step_pct,
  ROUND(100.0 * users * 1.0 / FIRST_VALUE(users) OVER (ORDER BY step), 1) AS pct_of_top
FROM counts
ORDER BY step;

title: Gmail Label Auto-Classification Hit Rate
solution:
SELECT predicted_label, actual_label,
  COUNT(*) AS messages,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*)
              / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (PARTITION BY actual_label), 1) AS pct_of_actual
FROM email_labels
GROUP BY predicted_label, actual_label
ORDER BY actual_label, messages DESC;

title: Order Fulfillment SLA — On-Time Delivery %
schema: orders(order_id, ordered_at, promised_at, delivered_at, marketplace, customer_id, order_date, status, amount)
solution:
SELECT marketplace,
  COUNT(*) AS orders,
  SUM(CASE WHEN (delivered_at) <= (promised_at) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS on_time,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN (delivered_at) <= (promised_at) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS on_time_pct,
  ROUND(AVG((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (delivered_at - ordered_at)) / 86400.0) * 24), 1) AS avg_delivery_hours
FROM orders
WHERE delivered_at IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY marketplace
ORDER BY on_time_pct DESC;

title: Prime Conversion Funnel — Trial → Paid → Renewed
solution:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS trials,
  COUNT(trial_converted) AS converted,
  COUNT(first_renewal) AS renewed,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(trial_converted) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS trial_to_paid_pct,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(first_renewal)   / NULLIF(COUNT(trial_converted), 0), 1) AS paid_to_renew_pct,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(first_renewal)   / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS overall_renewal_pct
FROM prime_subs;

title: Catalog LAD — ASIN with Late-Arriving Metadata
schema: orders(order_id, asin, ordered_at, customer_id, order_date, status, amount), asin_dim(asin, title, category, effective_date)
solution:
WITH current_dim AS (
  SELECT asin, title, category,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY asin ORDER BY effective_date DESC) AS rn
  FROM asin_dim
)
SELECT o.order_id, o.asin,
  COALESCE(d.title,    'ASIN ' || o.asin || ' (Pending Catalog Sync)') AS title,
  COALESCE(d.category, 'Uncategorized') AS category,
  o.ordered_at
FROM orders o
LEFT JOIN current_dim d
  ON d.asin = o.asin AND d.rn = 1
ORDER BY o.ordered_at DESC;

title: Return-Rate by Category with Reason Mix
schema: orders(order_id, asin, category, customer_id, order_date, status, amount), returns(return_id, order_id, reason)
solution:
SELECT o.category,
  COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS orders,
  COUNT(DISTINCT r.return_id) AS returns,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT r.return_id) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id), 0), 1) AS return_rate_pct
FROM orders o
LEFT JOIN returns r ON r.order_id = o.order_id
GROUP BY o.category
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) >= 5
ORDER BY return_rate_pct DESC;

title: Multi-Warehouse Inventory Routing — Closest-FC Wins
schema: orders(order_id, customer_zone, ordered_at, customer_id, order_date, status, amount), inventory(asin, warehouse_id, zone, qty_on_hand)
solution:
WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT o.order_id, o.customer_zone, i.warehouse_id, i.zone AS warehouse_zone, i.qty_on_hand,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY o.order_id
      ORDER BY CASE WHEN o.customer_zone = i.zone THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
               i.qty_on_hand DESC
    ) AS pick_order
  FROM orders o
  JOIN inventory i ON i.qty_on_hand > 0
)
SELECT order_id, customer_zone, warehouse_id, warehouse_zone, qty_on_hand
FROM ranked
WHERE pick_order = 1;

title: Recommendation A/B Test — Lift in Add-to-Cart Rate
solution:
SELECT bucket,
  COUNT(*) AS impressions,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN clicked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN clicked::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS ctr_pct,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN added_to_cart::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN added_to_cart::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS atc_pct,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN added_to_cart::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN added_to_cart::int ELSE 0 END) /
    NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN clicked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN clicked::int ELSE 0 END), 0), 2) AS atc_per_click_pct
FROM ab_events
GROUP BY bucket
ORDER BY bucket;

title: FBA Seller Performance — Stockout Days per Month
solution:
SELECT seller_id,
  TO_CHAR(snapshot_date::date, 'YYYY-MM') AS month,
  COUNT(DISTINCT snapshot_date) AS days_observed,
  SUM(CASE WHEN qty_on_hand = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS stockout_days,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN qty_on_hand = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS stockout_rate_pct
FROM inventory_snapshots
GROUP BY seller_id, TO_CHAR(snapshot_date::date, 'YYYY-MM')
ORDER BY stockout_rate_pct DESC;

title: Sponsored Products Attribution — Click-Through vs View-Through
schema: ad_impressions(impression_id, user_id, asin, shown_at, clicked), purchases(user_id, asin, purchased_at)
solution:
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN i.clicked::text = '1'
       AND EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (p.purchased_at - i.shown_at))/86400.0 BETWEEN 0 AND 7
       THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS click_through_attr,
  SUM(CASE WHEN i.clicked::text = '0'
       AND EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (p.purchased_at - i.shown_at))/86400.0 BETWEEN 0 AND 1
       THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS view_through_attr,
  COUNT(*) AS attributed_pairs
FROM ad_impressions i
JOIN purchases p ON p.user_id = i.user_id AND p.asin = i.asin
  AND p.purchased_at > i.shown_at
  AND (p.purchased_at - i.shown_at) <= INTERVAL '7 days';

title: App Store IAP Revenue by Category
solution:
SELECT category,
  COUNT(*) AS transactions,
  ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS gross_revenue,
  ROUND(SUM(amount) * 0.85, 2) AS dev_revenue,
  ROUND(SUM(amount) * 0.15, 2) AS apple_cut,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(amount) / SUM(SUM(amount)) OVER (), 1) AS share_of_total_pct
FROM iap_transactions
GROUP BY category
ORDER BY gross_revenue DESC;

title: iCloud Tier Upgrade Funnel
solution:
WITH transitions AS (
  SELECT user_id, tier,
    LAG(tier) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY upgraded_at) AS prev_tier
  FROM icloud_tiers
)
SELECT prev_tier, tier AS new_tier, COUNT(*) AS users
FROM transitions
WHERE prev_tier IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY prev_tier, tier
ORDER BY prev_tier, users DESC;

title: Device Upgrade Cycle — Months Between Purchases
solution:
WITH gaps AS (
  SELECT customer_id, device_model, purchased_at,
    LAG(purchased_at) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY purchased_at) AS prev_purchase
  FROM device_purchases
)
SELECT customer_id, device_model, purchased_at,
  CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (purchased_at - prev_purchase)) / 86400.0) / 30.0 AS INTEGER) AS months_since_last
FROM gaps
WHERE prev_purchase IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY months_since_last DESC;

title: Push Notification Delivery SLA
solution:
WITH latencies AS (
  SELECT app_id,
    CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (delivered_at - queued_at)) / 86400.0) * 86400 AS INTEGER) AS latency_seconds
  FROM push_events
  WHERE delivered_at IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT app_id,
  COUNT(*) AS deliveries,
  ROUND(AVG(latency_seconds), 1) AS avg_latency_sec,
  MAX(latency_seconds) AS max_latency_sec,
  SUM(CASE WHEN latency_seconds <= 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS within_5s_count,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN latency_seconds <= 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS within_5s_pct
FROM latencies
GROUP BY app_id
ORDER BY within_5s_pct DESC;

title: App Store Search CTR per Query Term
subtopic: CTR per group with sample-size gate
solution:
SELECT query_term,
  COUNT(*) AS impressions,
  SUM(CASE WHEN clicked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN clicked::int ELSE 0 END) AS clicks,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN clicked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN clicked::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS ctr_pct
FROM app_searches
GROUP BY query_term
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY ctr_pct DESC;

title: Refund Rate by App Category — Quality Signal
solution:
SELECT category,
  COUNT(*) AS purchases,
  SUM(CASE WHEN refunded::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN refunded::int ELSE 0 END) AS refunds,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN refunded::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN refunded::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS refund_rate_pct,
  ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN refunded::text = '1' THEN amount END)::numeric, 2) AS refunded_dollars
FROM iap_transactions
GROUP BY category
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5
ORDER BY refund_rate_pct DESC;

title: Payment Success Rate by Method + Country
schema: payments(payment_id, method, country, status, amount, customer_id, payment_date)
solution:
SELECT method, country,
  COUNT(*) AS attempts,
  SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'succeeded' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS successes,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'succeeded' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS success_rate_pct
FROM payments
GROUP BY method, country
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1
ORDER BY method, success_rate_pct DESC;

title: Velocity-Rule Fraud — 3+ Attempts Within 5 Minutes
solution:
SELECT a.user_id, a.attempted_at,
  COUNT(b.attempt_id) AS attempts_in_5min
FROM payment_attempts a
JOIN payment_attempts b
  ON b.user_id = a.user_id
 AND julianday(b.attempted_at) BETWEEN julianday(a.attempted_at) - 1.0/288 AND julianday(a.attempted_at)
GROUP BY a.user_id, a.attempted_at
HAVING COUNT(b.attempt_id) >= 3
ORDER BY a.user_id, a.attempted_at;

title: MRR Cohort Retention — Monthly Survival Curve
schema: subscriptions(customer_id, signup_month, active_month, subscription_id, plan, start_date, end_date, status)
solution:
WITH joined AS (
  SELECT signup_month,
    CASE
      WHEN signup_month::text ~ '^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}$' AND active_month::text ~ '^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}$'
        THEN ((EXTRACT(YEAR FROM (active_month||'-01')::date)::int - EXTRACT(YEAR FROM (signup_month||'-01')::date)::int) * 12
              + (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM (active_month||'-01')::date)::int - EXTRACT(MONTH FROM (signup_month||'-01')::date)::int))
      WHEN signup_month::text ~ '^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$' AND active_month::text ~ '^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$'
        THEN ((EXTRACT(YEAR FROM active_month::date)::int - EXTRACT(YEAR FROM signup_month::date)::int) * 12
              + (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM active_month::date)::int - EXTRACT(MONTH FROM signup_month::date)::int))
      ELSE NULL
    END AS month_n,
    customer_id
  FROM subscriptions
)
SELECT signup_month, month_n,
  COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS active_customers,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) /
    FIRST_VALUE(COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id)) OVER (PARTITION BY signup_month ORDER BY month_n), 1) AS retention_pct
FROM joined
WHERE month_n BETWEEN 0 AND 12
GROUP BY signup_month, month_n
ORDER BY signup_month, month_n;

title: Churn Waterfall — New / Existing / Churned / Resurrected
solution:
WITH classified AS (
  SELECT customer_id, month, mrr,
    LAG(mrr) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY month) AS prev_mrr
  FROM monthly_revenue
)
SELECT month,
  SUM(CASE WHEN prev_mrr IS NULL  AND mrr > 0          THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS new_customers,
  SUM(CASE WHEN prev_mrr > 0      AND mrr > 0          THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS existing,
  SUM(CASE WHEN prev_mrr > 0      AND (mrr = 0 OR mrr IS NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS churned,
  SUM(CASE WHEN prev_mrr = 0      AND mrr > 0          THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS resurrected
FROM classified
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month;

title: Dispute Rate per Merchant — Risk Scoring
solution:
SELECT merchant_id,
  COUNT(*) AS charges,
  SUM(CASE WHEN disputed::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN disputed::int ELSE 0 END) AS disputes,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN disputed::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN disputed::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS dispute_rate_pct,
  CASE
    WHEN 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN disputed::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN disputed::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) >= 1.0 THEN 'high_risk'
    WHEN 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN disputed::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN disputed::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) >= 0.3 THEN 'monitor'
    ELSE 'normal'
  END AS risk_tier
FROM charges
GROUP BY merchant_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5
ORDER BY dispute_rate_pct DESC;

title: Payout Reconciliation — Charges − Refunds = Payout
schema: charges(charge_id, merchant_id, amount, charged_at), refunds(refund_id, charge_id, amount, refunded_at), payouts(payout_id, merchant_id, amount, paid_at)
solution:
WITH net AS (
  SELECT c.merchant_id,
    ROUND(SUM(c.amount), 2) AS gross_charges,
    ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(r.amount), 0), 2) AS gross_refunds,
    ROUND(SUM(c.amount) - COALESCE(SUM(r.amount), 0), 2) AS expected_payout
  FROM charges c
  LEFT JOIN refunds r ON r.charge_id = c.charge_id
  GROUP BY c.merchant_id
),
combined AS (
  SELECT n.merchant_id, n.gross_charges, n.gross_refunds, n.expected_payout,
    ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(p.amount), 0), 2) AS actual_payout,
    ROUND(n.expected_payout - COALESCE(SUM(p.amount), 0), 2) AS reconciliation_diff
  FROM net n
  LEFT JOIN payouts p ON p.merchant_id = n.merchant_id
  GROUP BY n.merchant_id, n.gross_charges, n.gross_refunds, n.expected_payout
)
SELECT * FROM combined
ORDER BY ABS(reconciliation_diff) DESC;

title: Occupancy Rate by City — Booked Nights / Available Nights
solution:
SELECT city,
  COUNT(*) AS listing_nights,
  SUM(CASE WHEN booked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN booked::int ELSE 0 END) AS booked_nights,
  SUM(CASE WHEN available::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN available::int ELSE 0 END) AS available_nights,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN booked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN booked::int ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN available::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN available::int ELSE 0 END)
                      + SUM(CASE WHEN booked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN booked::int ELSE 0 END), 0), 1) AS occupancy_pct
FROM calendar_nights
GROUP BY city
ORDER BY occupancy_pct DESC;

title: Host Response Time — Median per Listing
solution:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS messages;
CREATE TABLE messages (
  message_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  listing_id INT,
  host_id INT,
  sent_by TEXT,
  sent_at TIMESTAMP
);
INSERT INTO messages VALUES
  (1, 10, 500, 'guest', '2024-04-01 09:00'),
  (2, 10, 500, 'host',  '2024-04-01 09:10'),
  (3, 10, 500, 'guest', '2024-04-01 10:00'),
  (4, 10, 500, 'host',  '2024-04-01 10:30'),
  (5, 10, 500, 'guest', '2024-04-01 11:00'),
  (6, 10, 500, 'host',  '2024-04-01 11:15'),
  (7, 20, 600, 'guest', '2024-04-02 08:00'),
  (8, 20, 600, 'host',  '2024-04-02 08:05'),
  (9, 20, 600, 'guest', '2024-04-02 09:00'),
  (10,20, 600, 'host',  '2024-04-02 09:20'),
  (11,20, 600, 'guest', '2024-04-02 10:00'),
  (12,20, 600, 'host',  '2024-04-02 10:45'),
  (13,30, 700, 'guest', '2024-04-03 07:00'),
  (14,30, 700, 'host',  '2024-04-03 07:30');

WITH guest_msgs AS (
  SELECT listing_id, sent_at AS guest_at,
    LEAD(sent_at) OVER (PARTITION BY listing_id ORDER BY sent_at) AS host_at,
    LEAD(sent_by) OVER (PARTITION BY listing_id ORDER BY sent_at) AS next_sender
  FROM messages
)
SELECT listing_id,
  COUNT(*) AS guest_messages,
  ROUND(AVG((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (host_at - guest_at)) / 86400.0) * 1440), 1) AS avg_response_minutes
FROM guest_msgs
WHERE next_sender = 'host'
GROUP BY listing_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY avg_response_minutes;

title: Search-to-Book Funnel by Property Type
schema: search_events(search_id, user_id, property_type, viewed_listing_id, searched_at), bookings(user_id, listing_id, booked_at)
solution:
SELECT s.property_type,
  COUNT(DISTINCT s.search_id) AS searches,
  COUNT(DISTINCT b.user_id) AS booked_users,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT b.user_id) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT s.user_id), 0), 1) AS user_book_pct
FROM search_events s
LEFT JOIN bookings b
  ON b.user_id = s.user_id
 AND b.listing_id = s.viewed_listing_id
 AND EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (b.booked_at - s.searched_at)) / 86400.0 BETWEEN 0 AND 30
GROUP BY s.property_type
ORDER BY user_book_pct DESC;

title: Superhost Eligibility — Multi-Criteria Boolean
solution:
SELECT host_id, completed_trips, avg_rating, response_rate_pct, cancellation_count,
  CASE
    WHEN (CASE WHEN completed_trips::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN completed_trips::numeric ELSE 0 END) >= 10
     AND (CASE WHEN avg_rating::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN avg_rating::numeric ELSE 0 END) >= 4.8
     AND (CASE WHEN response_rate_pct::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN response_rate_pct::numeric ELSE 0 END) >= 90
     AND (CASE WHEN cancellation_count::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN cancellation_count::int ELSE 0 END) = 0
    THEN 'superhost' ELSE 'not_eligible'
  END AS status
FROM host_metrics
ORDER BY status, avg_rating DESC;

title: Cancellation Rate by Stay Length
solution:
WITH nb AS (
  SELECT *, CASE WHEN nights::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN nights::int ELSE 0 END AS n_int
  FROM bookings
)
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN n_int <= 2  THEN '1-2 nights'
    WHEN n_int <= 6  THEN '3-6 nights'
    WHEN n_int <= 14 THEN '1-2 weeks'
    ELSE '15+ nights'
  END AS stay_band,
  COUNT(*) AS bookings,
  SUM(CASE WHEN cancelled::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN cancelled::int ELSE 0 END) AS cancellations,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN cancelled::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN cancelled::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS cancel_rate_pct
FROM nb
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY cancel_rate_pct DESC;

title: Calendar Gap Detection — Listings With Black-Out Stretches
solution:
WITH unavail AS (
  SELECT listing_id, night,
    julianday(night) - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY listing_id ORDER BY night) AS island_id
  FROM calendar_nights
  WHERE available = 0
)
SELECT listing_id,
  MIN(night) AS blackout_start,
  MAX(night) AS blackout_end,
  COUNT(*) AS blackout_days
FROM unavail
GROUP BY listing_id, island_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1
ORDER BY blackout_days DESC;

title: Song Skip Rate by Genre
schema: plays(play_id, user_id, song_id, genre, played_seconds, song_seconds, skipped)
solution:
SELECT genre,
  COUNT(*) AS plays,
  SUM(CASE WHEN skipped::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN skipped::int ELSE 0 END) AS skips,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN skipped::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN skipped::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS skip_rate_pct,
  ROUND(AVG(played_seconds * 100.0 / NULLIF(song_seconds, 0)), 1) AS avg_completion_pct
FROM plays
GROUP BY genre
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY skip_rate_pct DESC;

title: Playlist Engagement — Listens / Saves / Shares
solution:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS playlist_actions;
CREATE TABLE playlist_actions (playlist_id INT, user_id INT, action_type TEXT);
INSERT INTO playlist_actions (playlist_id, user_id, action_type) VALUES
  (1, 101, 'listen'), (1, 102, 'listen'), (1, 103, 'listen'),
  (1, 101, 'save'),  (1, 104, 'save'),
  (1, 102, 'share'),
  (2, 201, 'listen'), (2, 202, 'listen'), (2, 203, 'listen'), (2, 204, 'listen'),
  (2, 201, 'save'),
  (2, 205, 'share'), (2, 206, 'share'),
  (3, 301, 'listen'), (3, 302, 'listen'),
  (3, 301, 'save'),
  (4, 401, 'listen'), (4, 402, 'listen'), (4, 403, 'listen'),
  (4, 401, 'save'), (4, 402, 'save'), (4, 403, 'save');
SELECT playlist_id,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'listen' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS listens,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'save'   THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS saves,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'share'  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS shares,
  COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unique_users,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'save' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'listen' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0), 1) AS save_per_listen_pct
FROM playlist_actions
GROUP BY playlist_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY save_per_listen_pct DESC;

title: Discover Weekly Discovery Rate — Tracks Saved After First Play
schema: discover_plays(user_id, song_id, played_at), library_saves(user_id, song_id, saved_at)
solution:
SELECT
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.user_id || '|' || d.song_id) AS discovered_pairs,
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN s.song_id IS NOT NULL THEN d.user_id || '|' || d.song_id END) AS saved_pairs,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN s.song_id IS NOT NULL THEN d.user_id || '|' || d.song_id END)
              / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT d.user_id || '|' || d.song_id), 0), 1) AS discovery_save_rate_pct
FROM discover_plays d
LEFT JOIN library_saves s
  ON s.user_id = d.user_id
 AND s.song_id = d.song_id
 AND (s.saved_at) >= (d.played_at)
 AND (s.saved_at - d.played_at) <= INTERVAL '7 days';

title: Podcast Episode Completion Rate
solution:
WITH banded AS (
  SELECT episode_id,
    CASE
      WHEN listened_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(episode_seconds, 0) >= 0.95 THEN 'completed'
      WHEN listened_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(episode_seconds, 0) >= 0.50 THEN 'engaged'
      WHEN listened_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(episode_seconds, 0) >= 0.10 THEN 'sampled'
      ELSE 'bounced'
    END AS engagement_band
  FROM podcast_plays
)
SELECT episode_id, engagement_band, COUNT(*) AS plays
FROM banded
GROUP BY episode_id, engagement_band
ORDER BY episode_id, plays DESC;

title: Premium Trial Conversion by Country
solution:
SELECT country,
  COUNT(*) AS trials,
  COUNT(converted_to_paid) AS converted,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(converted_to_paid) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS conversion_pct,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN cancelled_at IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS cancelled
FROM trials
GROUP BY country
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY conversion_pct DESC;

title: Royalty Pool Pro-Rata — Streams × Country Rate
schema: streams(stream_id, artist_id, country, played_at), country_pool(country, monthly_pool_usd)
solution:
WITH streams_per_artist AS (
  SELECT s.country, s.artist_id, COUNT(*) AS streams
  FROM streams s GROUP BY s.country, s.artist_id
),
country_total AS (
  SELECT country, SUM(streams) AS total_streams
  FROM streams_per_artist GROUP BY country
),
pool_clean AS (
  SELECT country,
    CASE WHEN monthly_pool_usd::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN monthly_pool_usd::numeric ELSE 0 END AS monthly_pool_usd
  FROM country_pool
)
SELECT spa.country, spa.artist_id, spa.streams, ct.total_streams,
  ROUND(spa.streams * 1.0 / NULLIF(ct.total_streams, 0) * cp.monthly_pool_usd, 2) AS payout_usd
FROM streams_per_artist spa
JOIN country_total ct ON ct.country = spa.country
JOIN pool_clean cp ON cp.country = spa.country
ORDER BY payout_usd DESC;

title: Family Plan Member Utilization
solution:
SELECT fp.account_id, fp.max_members,
  COUNT(DISTINCT fl.member_id) AS active_members,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT fl.member_id) / NULLIF(fp.max_members, 0), 1) AS utilization_pct,
  ROUND(SUM(fl.listened_minutes) * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT fl.member_id), 0), 0) AS avg_minutes_per_member
FROM family_plans fp
LEFT JOIN family_listening fl ON fl.account_id = fp.account_id
GROUP BY fp.account_id, fp.max_members
ORDER BY utilization_pct DESC;

title: Audio Quality Bitrate Drops — Per Session
solution:
WITH transitions AS (
  SELECT session_id, event_at, bitrate_kbps,
    LAG(bitrate_kbps) OVER (PARTITION BY session_id ORDER BY event_at) AS prev_bitrate
  FROM playback_events
)
SELECT session_id,
  COUNT(*) AS events,
  SUM(CASE WHEN prev_bitrate IS NOT NULL AND bitrate_kbps != prev_bitrate THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS quality_changes,
  MIN(bitrate_kbps) AS lowest_bitrate,
  MAX(bitrate_kbps) AS highest_bitrate
FROM transitions
GROUP BY session_id
ORDER BY quality_changes DESC;

title: Dasher Utilization — Active Time / Online Time
schema: dasher_shifts(dasher_id, online_at, offline_at), deliveries(dasher_id, started_at, ended_at)
solution:
SELECT s.dasher_id,
  ROUND(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (s.offline_at - s.online_at)) / 86400.0) * 24.0, 2) AS hours_online,
  ROUND(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.ended_at - d.started_at)) / 86400.0) * 24.0, 2) AS hours_delivering,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.ended_at - d.started_at)) / 86400.0)
              / NULLIF(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (s.offline_at - s.online_at)) / 86400.0), 0), 1) AS utilization_pct
FROM dasher_shifts s
LEFT JOIN deliveries d ON d.dasher_id = s.dasher_id
GROUP BY s.dasher_id
ORDER BY utilization_pct DESC;

title: Delivery Time SLA — Within 30 Minutes
schema: orders(order_id, ordered_at, delivered_at, market, customer_id, order_date, status, amount)
solution:
SELECT market,
  COUNT(*) AS deliveries,
  SUM(CASE WHEN (EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (delivered_at - ordered_at)) / 86400.0) * 1440 <= 30 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS within_30min,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN (EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (delivered_at - ordered_at)) / 86400.0) * 1440 <= 30 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS sla_pct,
  ROUND(AVG((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (delivered_at - ordered_at)) / 86400.0) * 1440), 1) AS avg_minutes
FROM orders
WHERE delivered_at IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY market
ORDER BY sla_pct DESC;

title: Restaurant Performance — Volume × Rating Composite
schema: restaurants(restaurant_id, name, market, cuisine, city), orders(order_id, restaurant_id, rating, customer_id, order_date, status, amount)
solution:
SELECT r.restaurant_id, r.name, r.market,
  COUNT(o.order_id) AS orders,
  ROUND(AVG(o.rating), 2) AS avg_rating,
  ROUND(AVG(o.rating) * 0.7 + (COUNT(o.order_id) * 1.0 / 100) * 0.3, 2) AS composite_score
FROM restaurants r
LEFT JOIN orders o ON o.restaurant_id = r.restaurant_id
GROUP BY r.restaurant_id, r.name, r.market
HAVING COUNT(o.order_id) >= 3
ORDER BY composite_score DESC;

title: Multi-Order Stacking — Two Orders Same Dasher Same Hour
solution:
SELECT a.dasher_id, a.delivery_id AS order_a, b.delivery_id AS order_b,
  CAST(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (b.started_at - a.started_at))/60 AS INTEGER) AS minutes_apart
FROM deliveries a
JOIN deliveries b
  ON b.dasher_id = a.dasher_id
 AND b.delivery_id != a.delivery_id
 AND b.started_at > a.started_at
 AND (b.started_at - a.started_at) <= INTERVAL '1 hour'
ORDER BY a.dasher_id, a.started_at;

title: Surge Multiplier Effectiveness — Lift Above Normal Volume
schema: orders(order_id, market, ordered_at, fare, surge_multiplier, customer_id, order_date, status, amount)
solution:
SELECT market,
  CASE
    WHEN surge_multiplier <  1.3 THEN 'normal'
    WHEN surge_multiplier <  1.7 THEN 'mild'
    ELSE 'heavy'
  END AS surge_band,
  COUNT(*) AS orders,
  ROUND(AVG(fare), 2) AS avg_fare,
  ROUND(SUM(fare), 2) AS total_revenue
FROM orders
GROUP BY market, surge_band
ORDER BY market, surge_band;

title: Customer Cohort LTV — 90-Day Spend per Acquisition Month
schema: orders(customer_id, ordered_at, total, order_id, order_date, status, amount)
solution:
WITH cohorts AS (
  SELECT customer_id, MIN(ordered_at) AS first_order_at
  FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id
),
spend_in_window AS (
  SELECT c.customer_id,
    TO_CHAR(c.first_order_at::date, 'YYYY-MM') AS cohort_month,
    SUM(o.total) AS cumulative_spend
  FROM cohorts c
  JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
    AND (o.ordered_at::date - c.first_order_at::date) BETWEEN 0 AND 90
  GROUP BY c.customer_id, TO_CHAR(c.first_order_at::date, 'YYYY-MM')
)
SELECT cohort_month,
  COUNT(*) AS new_customers,
  ROUND(AVG(cumulative_spend), 2) AS avg_90d_ltv,
  ROUND(SUM(cumulative_spend), 2) AS cohort_total_spend
FROM spend_in_window
GROUP BY cohort_month
ORDER BY cohort_month;

title: Order Rejection Rate by Restaurant Tier
subtopic: Rate per tier with sample-size gate
solution:
SELECT tier,
  COUNT(*) AS offers,
  SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'rejected' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS rejected,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'rejected' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS rejection_rate_pct
FROM order_offers
GROUP BY tier
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY rejection_rate_pct DESC;

title: Tip Pattern by Order Size + Time of Day
schema: orders(order_id, total, tip, ordered_at, customer_id, order_date, status, amount)
solution:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN total < 25 THEN 'small'
    WHEN total < 60 THEN 'medium'
    ELSE 'large'
  END AS order_band,
  CASE
    WHEN CAST(TO_CHAR(ordered_at, 'HH24') AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 17 AND 21 THEN 'dinner'
    WHEN CAST(TO_CHAR(ordered_at, 'HH24') AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 11 AND 14 THEN 'lunch'
    ELSE 'offpeak'
  END AS daypart,
  COUNT(*) AS orders,
  ROUND(AVG(tip), 2) AS avg_tip,
  ROUND(100.0 * AVG(tip * 1.0 / NULLIF(total, 0)), 1) AS tip_pct_of_total
FROM orders
GROUP BY order_band, daypart
ORDER BY order_band, daypart;

title: Connection-of-Connection — 2-Hop Suggestions
solution:
SELECT a.member_id AS me, b.connection_id AS suggested,
  COUNT(*) AS shared_connections
FROM connections a
JOIN connections b
  ON a.connection_id = b.member_id
 AND a.member_id != b.connection_id
GROUP BY a.member_id, b.connection_id
ORDER BY shared_connections DESC, me, suggested
LIMIT 30;

title: InMail Response Rate by Recipient Industry
schema: inmails(inmail_id, sender_id, recipient_id, sent_at, replied), members(member_id, industry)
solution:
SELECT m.industry,
  COUNT(*) AS inmails,
  SUM(CASE WHEN i.replied::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN i.replied::int ELSE 0 END) AS replies,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN i.replied::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN i.replied::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS response_rate_pct
FROM inmails i
JOIN members m ON m.member_id = i.recipient_id
GROUP BY m.industry
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY response_rate_pct DESC;

title: Job Application Funnel — Saved → Applied → Recruiter-Viewed
solution:
WITH per_pair AS (
  SELECT member_id, job_id,
    SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'saved'   THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS saved,
    SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'applied' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS applied,
    SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'recruiter_viewed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS recruiter_viewed
  FROM job_actions
  GROUP BY member_id, job_id
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS member_job_pairs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN saved >= 1   THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS saved_step,
  SUM(CASE WHEN applied >= 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS applied_step,
  SUM(CASE WHEN recruiter_viewed >= 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS recruiter_viewed_step,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN applied >= 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN saved >= 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0), 1) AS save_to_apply_pct
FROM per_pair;

title: Feed Post Virality Score — Reactions / Comments / Reshares
solution:
SELECT post_id, author_id,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'reaction'  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS reactions,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'comment'   THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS comments,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'reshare'   THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS reshares,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'reaction'  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1
  + SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'comment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 3
  + SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'reshare' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 5 AS virality_score
FROM post_engagements
GROUP BY post_id, author_id
ORDER BY virality_score DESC;

title: Recruiter Daily Search Volume
solution:
WITH per_day AS (
  SELECT recruiter_id, DATE(searched_at) AS day, COUNT(*) AS searches
  FROM recruiter_searches
  GROUP BY recruiter_id, DATE(searched_at)
)
SELECT recruiter_id, day, searches,
  SUM(searches) OVER (PARTITION BY recruiter_id) AS total_searches,
  COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY recruiter_id) AS active_days
FROM per_day
ORDER BY recruiter_id, day;

title: Premium Plan Upgrade Funnel
solution:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS viewers,
  COUNT(started_trial_at) AS started_trial,
  COUNT(converted_at) AS converted,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(started_trial_at) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS view_to_trial_pct,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(converted_at) / NULLIF(COUNT(started_trial_at), 0), 1) AS trial_to_paid_pct,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(converted_at) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS view_to_paid_pct
FROM premium_funnel;

title: Network Density per Industry — Avg Connections
Post Engagement by Type — Articles vs Updates vs Videos
solution:
WITH p AS (
  SELECT post_type,
    CASE WHEN impressions::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN impressions::int ELSE 0 END AS impressions,
    CASE WHEN engagements::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN engagements::int ELSE 0 END AS engagements
  FROM posts
)
SELECT post_type,
  COUNT(*) AS posts,
  SUM(impressions) AS total_impressions,
  SUM(engagements) AS total_engagements,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(engagements) / NULLIF(SUM(impressions), 0), 2) AS engagement_rate_pct,
  ROUND(SUM(engagements) * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS avg_engagements_per_post
FROM p
GROUP BY post_type
ORDER BY engagement_rate_pct DESC;

title: Story View Completion per Snap
solution:
SELECT story_id,
  COUNT(*) AS impressions,
  SUM(CASE WHEN viewed::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN viewed::int ELSE 0 END) AS views,
  SUM(CASE WHEN swiped_through::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN swiped_through::int ELSE 0 END) AS swiped_away,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN swiped_through::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN swiped_through::int ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN viewed::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN viewed::int ELSE 0 END), 0), 1) AS swipe_through_pct
FROM story_snaps
GROUP BY story_id
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN viewed::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN viewed::int ELSE 0 END) >= 3
ORDER BY swipe_through_pct;

title: Snapstreak Detection — Consecutive Days Both Sides Sent
solution:
WITH islands AS (
  SELECT user_a, user_b, exchange_date,
    julianday(exchange_date) - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_a, user_b ORDER BY exchange_date) AS island_id
  FROM snap_exchanges
)
SELECT user_a, user_b,
  MIN(exchange_date) AS streak_start,
  MAX(exchange_date) AS streak_end,
  COUNT(*) AS streak_days
FROM islands
GROUP BY user_a, user_b, island_id
ORDER BY streak_days DESC;

title: AR Filter Usage — Top Filters by Send Volume
solution:
SELECT filter_id,
  COUNT(*) AS uses,
  COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unique_users,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 1) AS share_of_uses_pct
FROM filter_usage
GROUP BY filter_id
ORDER BY uses DESC
LIMIT 10;

title: Snap Map Active Locations — Per-Hour Density
solution:
SELECT geohash,
  CAST(TO_CHAR(recorded_at, 'HH24') AS INTEGER) AS hour,
  COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS active_users
FROM map_activity
GROUP BY geohash, hour
ORDER BY active_users DESC
LIMIT 20;

title: Discover Channel Engagement — Subscribers vs Repeat Viewers
solution:
SELECT channel_id,
  COUNT(*) AS views,
  COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unique_viewers,
  SUM(is_subscriber) AS subscriber_views,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(is_subscriber) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS subscriber_share_pct
FROM discover_views
GROUP BY channel_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY views DESC;

title: Pin Save Rate by Category
solution:
SELECT category,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'view' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS views,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'save' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS saves,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'save' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'view' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0), 2) AS save_rate_pct
FROM pin_events
GROUP BY category
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'view' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) >= 3
ORDER BY save_rate_pct DESC;

title: Search Intent — Visual Query vs Text Query CTR
solution:
SELECT query_type,
  COUNT(*) AS searches,
  SUM(CASE WHEN clicked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN clicked::int ELSE 0 END) AS clicks,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN clicked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN clicked::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS ctr_pct
FROM searches
GROUP BY query_type
ORDER BY ctr_pct DESC;

title: Board Completion Cohort — Pins Saved per Board Age
schema: boards(board_id, owner_id, created_at), board_pins(board_id, pin_id, saved_at)
solution:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM ('2024-04-01' - b.created_at)) / 86400.0) / 30 AS INTEGER) <= 1 THEN 'new (<= 1mo)'
    WHEN CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM ('2024-04-01' - b.created_at)) / 86400.0) / 30 AS INTEGER) <= 6 THEN '1-6 mo'
    WHEN CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM ('2024-04-01' - b.created_at)) / 86400.0) / 30 AS INTEGER) <= 12 THEN '6-12 mo'
    ELSE '12+ mo'
  END AS board_age_band,
  COUNT(DISTINCT b.board_id) AS boards,
  COUNT(bp.pin_id) AS total_pins,
  ROUND(COUNT(bp.pin_id) * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT b.board_id), 0), 1) AS avg_pins_per_board
FROM boards b
LEFT JOIN board_pins bp ON bp.board_id = b.board_id
GROUP BY board_age_band
ORDER BY avg_pins_per_board DESC;

title: Promoted Pin CTR by Pin Type
solution:
SELECT pin_format,
  COUNT(*) AS impressions,
  SUM(CASE WHEN clicked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN clicked::int ELSE 0 END) AS clicks,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN clicked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN clicked::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS ctr_pct
FROM promoted_pins
GROUP BY pin_format
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY ctr_pct DESC;

title: Idea Pin Watch-Time Distribution
solution:
WITH banded AS (
  SELECT pin_id,
    CASE
      WHEN watched_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(total_seconds, 0) >= 0.95 THEN 'completed'
      WHEN watched_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(total_seconds, 0) >= 0.50 THEN 'engaged'
      WHEN watched_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(total_seconds, 0) >= 0.10 THEN 'sampled'
      ELSE 'bounced'
    END AS band
  FROM idea_pin_views
)
SELECT band, COUNT(*) AS plays,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 1) AS pct
FROM banded
GROUP BY band
ORDER BY plays DESC;

title: Lead → Opportunity Conversion Rate by Source
solution:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS leads;
CREATE TABLE leads (
  lead_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  source TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMP,
  converted_at TIMESTAMP
);
INSERT INTO leads (lead_id, source, created_at, converted_at) VALUES
  (1,  'web',     '2025-01-01 10:00:00', '2025-01-05 10:00:00'),
  (2,  'web',     '2025-01-02 10:00:00', '2025-01-10 10:00:00'),
  (3,  'web',     '2025-01-03 10:00:00', NULL),
  (4,  'web',     '2025-01-04 10:00:00', '2025-01-08 10:00:00'),
  (5,  'web',     '2025-01-05 10:00:00', NULL),
  (6,  'partner', '2025-01-06 10:00:00', '2025-01-09 10:00:00'),
  (7,  'partner', '2025-01-07 10:00:00', '2025-01-10 10:00:00'),
  (8,  'partner', '2025-01-08 10:00:00', '2025-01-12 10:00:00'),
  (9,  'partner', '2025-01-09 10:00:00', NULL),
  (10, 'event',   '2025-01-10 10:00:00', NULL),
  (11, 'event',   '2025-01-11 10:00:00', NULL),
  (12, 'event',   '2025-01-12 10:00:00', '2025-01-30 10:00:00');

SELECT source,
  COUNT(*) AS leads,
  COUNT(converted_at) AS converted,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(converted_at) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS conversion_pct,
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN converted_at IS NOT NULL
                 THEN (EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (converted_at - created_at)) / 86400.0) END)::numeric, 1) AS avg_days_to_convert
FROM leads
GROUP BY source
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY conversion_pct DESC;

title: Pipeline Velocity — Stage Duration per Opportunity
solution:
WITH transitions AS (
  SELECT opp_id, stage, entered_at,
    LEAD(entered_at) OVER (PARTITION BY opp_id ORDER BY entered_at) AS exited_at
  FROM opp_stages
)
SELECT stage,
  COUNT(*) AS transitions,
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN exited_at IS NOT NULL
                 THEN (EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (exited_at - entered_at)) / 86400.0) END), 1) AS avg_days_in_stage
FROM transitions
GROUP BY stage
ORDER BY avg_days_in_stage DESC;

title: MQL → SQL → Won Funnel Per Quarter
solution:
SELECT
  SUBSTRING(mql_at::text FROM 1 FOR 7) AS quarter_proxy,
  COUNT(*) AS mqls,
  COUNT(sql_at) AS sqls,
  COUNT(won_at) AS won,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(sql_at) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS mql_to_sql_pct,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(won_at) / NULLIF(COUNT(sql_at), 0), 1) AS sql_to_won_pct,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(won_at) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS overall_pct
FROM opportunities
GROUP BY SUBSTRING(mql_at::text FROM 1 FOR 7)
ORDER BY quarter_proxy;

title: Account Hierarchy — Recursive Parent-Child Rollup
schema: accounts(account_id, name, parent_account_id, arr, plan, signup_date, country)
solution:
WITH RECURSIVE chain AS (
  SELECT account_id, name, parent_account_id, arr, account_id AS root_id, 1 AS depth
  FROM accounts WHERE parent_account_id IS NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT a.account_id, a.name, a.parent_account_id, a.arr, c.root_id, c.depth + 1
  FROM accounts a JOIN chain c ON a.parent_account_id = c.account_id
)
SELECT root_id, MAX(depth) AS tree_depth, COUNT(*) AS total_accounts,
  ROUND(SUM(arr), 2) AS rolled_up_arr
FROM chain
GROUP BY root_id
ORDER BY rolled_up_arr DESC;

title: Win-Rate by Sales Rep — Top vs Bottom Quartile
solution:
WITH per_rep AS (
  SELECT owner_id,
    COUNT(*) AS opps,
    SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'won' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS wins,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'won' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
                / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS win_rate_pct
  FROM opportunities
  GROUP BY owner_id
  HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
)
SELECT owner_id, opps, wins, win_rate_pct,
  NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY win_rate_pct DESC) AS quartile
FROM per_rep
ORDER BY win_rate_pct DESC;

title: Stale Opportunities — No Activity in 30+ Days
solution:
WITH last_activity AS (
  SELECT opp_id, MAX(activity_at) AS most_recent
  FROM opp_activities
  GROUP BY opp_id
)
SELECT opp_id, most_recent,
  CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM ('2024-04-01' - most_recent)) / 86400.0) AS INTEGER) AS days_idle,
  CASE
    WHEN ('2024-04-01' - most_recent) > INTERVAL '60 days' THEN 'critical_stale'
    WHEN ('2024-04-01' - most_recent) > INTERVAL '30 days' THEN 'stale'
    ELSE 'active'
  END AS health
FROM last_activity
ORDER BY days_idle DESC;

title: Customer Health Score — Composite
solution:
WITH s AS (
  SELECT customer_id, login_30d_count, support_tickets_open, nps, last_renewal_pct,
    CASE WHEN login_30d_count::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN login_30d_count::numeric ELSE 0 END AS l_n,
    CASE WHEN support_tickets_open::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN support_tickets_open::numeric ELSE 0 END AS s_t,
    CASE WHEN nps::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN nps::numeric ELSE 0 END AS n_v,
    CASE WHEN last_renewal_pct::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN last_renewal_pct::numeric ELSE 0 END AS lr
  FROM customer_signals
)
SELECT customer_id, login_30d_count, support_tickets_open, nps, last_renewal_pct,
  ROUND(
    LEAST(l_n * 1.0 / 10, 1.0) * 30
    + (CASE WHEN s_t <= 1 THEN 25 WHEN s_t <= 3 THEN 15 ELSE 0 END)
    + (n_v + 100) / 200.0 * 25
    + lr * 0.20
  , 1) AS health_score
FROM s
ORDER BY health_score DESC;

title: Forecast Accuracy — Predicted vs Actual Close
solution:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS opps,
  ROUND(AVG(forecast_amount - actual_amount), 2) AS avg_bias,
  ROUND(AVG(ABS(forecast_amount - actual_amount)), 2) AS mae,
  ROUND(100.0 * AVG(ABS(forecast_amount - actual_amount) / NULLIF(actual_amount, 0)), 1) AS mape_pct,
  ROUND(SUM(forecast_amount), 2) AS total_forecast,
  ROUND(SUM(actual_amount), 2) AS total_actual
FROM forecasts
WHERE actual_amount IS NOT NULL;

title: Video Watch Retention — Per-Second Drop-Off
solution:
WITH banded AS (
  SELECT video_id,
    CASE
      WHEN watched_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(total_seconds, 0) >= 0.95 THEN 'completed'
      WHEN watched_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(total_seconds, 0) >= 0.50 THEN 'engaged'
      WHEN watched_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(total_seconds, 0) >= 0.25 THEN 'sampled'
      ELSE 'bounced'
    END AS band
  FROM video_views
)
SELECT band, COUNT(*) AS plays,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 1) AS pct
FROM banded
GROUP BY band
ORDER BY plays DESC;

title: For-You-Page Recommendation Lift — Treatment vs Control
solution:
SELECT bucket,
  COUNT(*) AS views,
  COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unique_users,
  ROUND(AVG(watched_seconds), 1) AS avg_watch_seconds,
  ROUND(SUM(watched_seconds) * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT user_id), 0), 1) AS seconds_per_user
FROM fyp_experiments
GROUP BY bucket
ORDER BY bucket;

title: Creator Earnings by Tier — Creator Fund
solution:
WITH c AS (
  SELECT creator_id,
    CASE WHEN follower_count::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN follower_count::int ELSE 0 END AS follower_count,
    CASE WHEN total_views::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN total_views::int ELSE 0 END AS total_views,
    CASE WHEN earnings::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN earnings::numeric ELSE 0 END AS earnings
  FROM creators
)
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN follower_count >= 1000000 THEN 'mega (1M+)'
    WHEN follower_count >= 100000 THEN 'macro (100K+)'
    WHEN follower_count >= 10000 THEN 'mid (10K+)'
    WHEN follower_count >= 1000 THEN 'micro (1K+)'
    ELSE 'nano'
  END AS tier,
  COUNT(*) AS creators,
  ROUND(AVG(earnings), 2) AS avg_earnings,
  ROUND(SUM(earnings), 2) AS total_earnings,
  ROUND(SUM(earnings) * 1000.0 / NULLIF(SUM(total_views), 0), 3) AS rpm
FROM c
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY avg_earnings DESC;

title: Sound Virality — Used by 1000+ Creators in 7 Days
solution:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS sound_uses;
CREATE TABLE sound_uses (
  use_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  sound_id INT,
  creator_id INT,
  used_at TIMESTAMP
);
INSERT INTO sound_uses VALUES
  (1,  100, 1,  '2024-03-26 09:00'),
  (2,  100, 2,  '2024-03-27 10:00'),
  (3,  100, 3,  '2024-03-28 11:00'),
  (4,  100, 4,  '2024-03-29 12:00'),
  (5,  100, 5,  '2024-03-30 13:00'),
  (6,  100, 6,  '2024-03-31 14:00'),
  (7,  200, 7,  '2024-03-27 08:00'),
  (8,  200, 8,  '2024-03-28 09:00'),
  (9,  200, 9,  '2024-03-29 10:00'),
  (10, 300, 10, '2024-03-30 09:00'),
  (11, 300, 10, '2024-03-31 10:00'),
  (12, 400, 11, '2024-02-01 09:00');

WITH window_uses AS (
  SELECT sound_id,
    COUNT(*) AS uses_7d,
    COUNT(DISTINCT creator_id) AS unique_creators_7d
  FROM sound_uses
  WHERE EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM ('2024-04-01'::timestamp - used_at)) / 86400.0 BETWEEN 0 AND 7
  GROUP BY sound_id
)
SELECT sound_id, uses_7d, unique_creators_7d,
  CASE WHEN unique_creators_7d >= 5 THEN 'viral' ELSE 'normal' END AS classification
FROM window_uses
ORDER BY unique_creators_7d DESC;

title: Live Stream Gift Revenue per Streamer
solution:
SELECT streamer_id,
  COUNT(*) AS gifts_received,
  COUNT(DISTINCT viewer_id) AS unique_gifters,
  SUM(gift_value_coins) AS total_coins,
  ROUND(SUM(gift_value_coins) / 100.0, 2) AS dollar_value,
  RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(gift_value_coins) DESC) AS revenue_rank
FROM live_gifts
GROUP BY streamer_id
ORDER BY total_coins DESC;

title: Hashtag Trend Detection — Velocity Increase
solution:
WITH weekly AS (
  SELECT hashtag_id,
    CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (used_at - '2024-01-01')) / 86400.0) / 7 AS INTEGER) AS week_n,
    COUNT(*) AS uses
  FROM hashtag_uses
  GROUP BY hashtag_id, week_n
)
SELECT hashtag_id, week_n, uses,
  LAG(uses) OVER (PARTITION BY hashtag_id ORDER BY week_n) AS prev_week,
  ROUND(100.0 * (uses - LAG(uses) OVER (PARTITION BY hashtag_id ORDER BY week_n))
              / NULLIF(LAG(uses) OVER (PARTITION BY hashtag_id ORDER BY week_n), 0), 1) AS wow_pct
FROM weekly
ORDER BY hashtag_id, week_n;

title: Comment Engagement Quality — Reply Depth + Like Volume
solution:
WITH RECURSIVE thread AS (
  SELECT comment_id, parent_id, video_id, likes, 1 AS depth
  FROM comments WHERE parent_id IS NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT c.comment_id, c.parent_id, c.video_id, c.likes, t.depth + 1
  FROM comments c JOIN thread t ON c.parent_id = t.comment_id
)
SELECT video_id,
  MAX(depth) AS max_thread_depth,
  COUNT(*) AS total_comments,
  SUM(likes) AS total_likes
FROM thread
GROUP BY video_id
ORDER BY max_thread_depth DESC;

title: Duet/Stitch Adoption — Original vs Derivative Volume
solution:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS videos;
CREATE TABLE videos (video_id INT, creator_id INT, parent_video_id INT, video_type TEXT);
INSERT INTO videos (video_id, creator_id, parent_video_id, video_type) VALUES
  (1, 10, NULL, 'original'),
  (2, 11, NULL, 'original'),
  (3, 12, NULL, 'original'),
  (4, 13, NULL, 'original'),
  (5, 20, 1, 'duet'),
  (6, 21, 1, 'duet'),
  (7, 22, 1, 'stitch'),
  (8, 23, 2, 'duet'),
  (9, 24, 2, 'stitch'),
  (10, 25, 2, 'stitch'),
  (11, 26, 3, 'duet');
SELECT v.video_id AS original_video,
  COUNT(d.video_id) AS derivatives,
  SUM(CASE WHEN d.video_type = 'duet'   THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS duets,
  SUM(CASE WHEN d.video_type = 'stitch' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS stitches
FROM videos v
LEFT JOIN videos d ON d.parent_video_id = v.video_id
WHERE v.parent_video_id IS NULL
GROUP BY v.video_id
ORDER BY derivatives DESC
LIMIT 20;

title: Post Karma Distribution — Top vs Median
solution:
SELECT subreddit_id,
